mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than not during month-over-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year rank correlation stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above recent levels signals weakening income growth and higher recession risk, which lowers expected earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes yield positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates resilient household demand and housing-led spending, which supports near-term revenues and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint-upturn episodes do not outperform single-series upturns, or if the 60-day forward return after joint strength is not positive in at least 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL signals improving real output without an inflation shock, which reduces growth scare risk and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month while CPIAUCSL inflation is not accelerating over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO surprises accompanied by stable CPIAUCSL do not deliver above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal turns negative in more than one major rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Very low FEDFUNDS below 1% keeps short-rate discounting compressed and encourages risk-taking, so the S&P 500 should post positive returns over the next 5 trading days when policy is pinned near zero.
failure_condition: Reject if below-1% policy episodes do not show positive 5-day forward returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the average triggered return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS level alongside a rising UNRATE confirms labor-market deterioration that weakens household spending expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint payroll loss and unemployment rise episodes fail to generate negative 60-day returns more often than not, or if the median return is not below the unconditional median.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in GDPC1 relative to recent quarters signals stronger aggregate demand and upward earnings revisions, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDPC1 growth does not correspond to above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the relationship flips across consecutive expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL acceleration stays muted while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real rates stay suppressed and equities should rise over the next 20 trading days; the effect fails if inflation reaccelerates or policy tightening begins.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation is flat or falling and FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if low-inflation, low-rate episodes do not outperform unconditional 20-day SPX returns, or if any subsequent CPIAUCSL reacceleration above the prior 3-month trend eliminates the signal.
